parentQuestion: Select the statement that is most correct.

scaffolds.q: A ganglion is a cluster of neuron cell bodies located outside the central nervous system.
scaffolds.explain: Ganglia (singular: ganglion) are clusters of nerve cell bodies in the peripheral nervous system. Inside the CNS, similar clusters are called nuclei. Sit with this knowledge:into this: same structure, different neighborhood, different name. The body uses location as taxonomy.

scaffolds.q: Afferent neurons carry signals TOWARD the central nervous system.
scaffolds.explain: Afferent means arriving, darling. Afferent (sensory) neurons carry information from receptors toward the CNS. Efferent (motor) neurons carry commands away from the CNS. Let me show you the elegance: the direction of signal flow defines the neuron type. Information inward, commands outward.
